chief_complaint: black tarry stools for 2 days and feeling dizzy
hpi_details: 3 episodes of melaena in 2 days, no haematemesis, dizziness on standing, mild epigastric discomfort, no frank PR bleeding
ideas: thinks it might be from eating too much iron-rich food
concerns: scared it could be bowel cancer
pmh: osteoarthritis (takes NSAIDs daily), no prior GI history
medications: ibuprofen 400mg TDS (taking for 6 months), omeprazole 20mg OD (recently started)
social_hx: non-smoker, occasional alcohol (3-4 units/week), works as a chef

reference_soap.subjective: 45M with 2-day history of melaena x3 episodes, orthostatic dizziness, mild epigastric pain. Takes NSAIDs daily for OA. No haematemesis, no frank PR bleeding.
reference_soap.objective: Likely upper GI bleed. Needs HR, BP (lying and standing), Hb, coagulation, urea (raised urea:creatinine ratio in upper GI bleed), group and screen.
reference_soap.assessment: Upper GI haemorrhage, likely peptic ulcer disease secondary to NSAID use. DDx: Mallory-Weiss tear, oesophagitis, gastric malignancy.
reference_soap.plan: Stop NSAIDs, IV access x2, IV PPI (80mg bolus then infusion), cross-match, urgent endoscopy (OGD) within 24hrs, surgical consult on standby, Blatchford score.

learning_objectives: Distinguish upper vs lower GI bleeding by symptom characterisation
Address ICE around cancer fear sensitively
